title: Doctors on Strike: How Hospitals Are Doing
summary: In England, doctors are on strike for six days to ask for more pay. The head of the NHS, Sir Jim Mackey, says hospitals are handling it well so far. Doctors who are still learning, called resident doctors, are not working. This is their 15th strike because they want better pay. To help, senior doctors are working extra in emergencies. Some planned surgeries had to be canceled, but most are still happening. The strike started after Easter, making it hard for hospitals. The doctors' group says the government did not offer a good deal. The government says they gave doctors a good pay raise and other help. Everyone hopes the strike ends soon.

the National Health Service, which runs hospitals in England

questions.question: How long is the doctors' strike in England?
What are senior doctors doing during the strike?
What did the government offer doctors?
options: A good pay raise and other help
questions.correct_answer: A good pay raise and other help

background_read: In England, the NHS is the public health system that provides free medical care. Doctors work in hospitals to treat patients. Sometimes, doctors go on strike, which means they stop working to ask for better pay or conditions. This can affect hospitals, as there are fewer doctors to help sick people. Senior doctors have more experience and can help during strikes. The government and doctors' groups talk to try to solve the problem. Strikes can lead to canceled appointments, but hospitals try to keep important services running.
Article_Structure: WHO: Doctors in England, especially resident doctors, and NHS head Sir Jim Mackey
WHAT: A six-day strike by doctors for better pay, with hospitals coping well so far
WHY: Because doctors want more pay and feel the government's offer is not good enough

perspectives.description: The NHS says hospitals are managing the strike well, but it's tough for staff.
Doctors say the strike is necessary because the government did not offer a fair deal.
The government says they offered doctors a generous deal with pay raises and other help.
